title: [1.18] Flaky unit test: TestSubscriptionRetryMechanisms/retry_stops_when_subscriber_is_closed on darwin
body:
## Summary

The unit test `TestSubscriptionRetryMechanisms/retry_stops_when_subscriber_is_closed` in `pkg/runtime/processor/subscriber` is flaky on darwin CI runners. It asserts a value is **strictly greater than** the same value, which is an off-by-one timing race in the retry-count tracking.

## Where I saw it

PR #9731 — https://github.com/dapr/dapr/pull/9731

Failing job (darwin unit tests):
https://github.com/dapr/dapr/actions/runs/25440059300/job/74629220512?pr=9731

The PR has nothing to do with the subscriber/retry code, so this is a pre-existing flake.

## Failure

```
=== FAIL: pkg/runtime/processor/subscriber TestSubscriptionRetryMechanisms/retry_stops_when_subscriber_is_closed (2.01s)
    subscriber_test.go:703:
        Error Trace: /Users/runner/work/dapr/dapr/pkg/runtime/processor/subscriber/subscriber_test.go:703
        Error:       "2" is not greater than "2"
        Test:        TestSubscriptionRetryMechanisms/retry_stops_when_subscriber_is_closed

=== FAIL: pkg/runtime/processor/subscriber TestSubscriptionRetryMechanisms (0.00s)
make: *** [test] Error 1
DONE 5727 tests, 4 skipped, 2 failures in 486.120s
```

## Diagnosis

The assertion `assert.Greater(t, count, 2)` is failing because the observed count is exactly 2 — the producer and assertion are racing. Either:
- the close path is short-circuiting one retry attempt that the test expected to land
- the goroutine that increments the counter is being descheduled long enough on slow runners that the third increment hasn't landed by the time the assertion fires

Likely fix: either `assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, count, 2)` if the test only cares about "at least one retry happened before close", or wait for the counter to settle (`assert.EventuallyWithT`) before snapshotting.

## Repro

CI darwin runs intermittently. Doesn't reproduce reliably locally. The PR linked above hit it on a vanilla CI run with no relevant code in the subscriber path.

comments.body: hi Can i please work on this

> hi Can i please work on this

Amazing!!! Thank you @devagya-rattan for your interest in the Dapr Open Source Project as well as this contribution! Pls lmk if you need anything along the way 🙌 🌞

thanks @sicoyle for assigning me this issue i will start working on this

can you please guide me how can i replicated this issue ?

hi @sicoyle 
I investigated the current code and noticed that the test now asserts finalCallCount > initialCallCount in the assert.greater function 
so the fix is to change the assert.greater function to assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, finalCallCount, initialCallCount) , in this way it will not cause ```"2" is not greater than "2"``` in the runner 
i would like you to confirm if it is the way??
